docs.title: PowerScale / OneFS — Operations Library
docs.text:
Last updated: 2026-03-09

Welcome to the PowerScale / OneFS Operations Library. This site collects day‑to‑day commands, job engine know‑how, FSA/IU recovery, drive triage, snapshot/quotas, CloudPools, and more.

Safety first → Read [[Risky Commands (Read First)]] before using any destructive command.

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• [[Most‑Used - Daily Cheat Sheet]]
  • [[Jobs & Job Engine (Core)]]
  • [[FSAnalyze & IndexUpdate (FSA-IU)]]
  • [[Drives - Health, Smartfail, Stalls & Boot Flash]]
  • [[Troubleshooting Playbooks]]

docs.title: Version awareness
docs.text: See [[Version Matrix (7.x → 9.7)]] for differences across releases.

docs.title: Auth — IIQ — SMB — NFS
docs.text:
egrep -i insightiq *-*/varlog.tar/log/apache2/webui_httpd_access.log | tail

isi smb openfiles list

docs.title: CloudPools
docs.text:
isi cloud accounts list -v
isi cloud jobs list -v

isi cloud recall <files> [-r]

docs.title: Cluster Health & Status
docs.text:
isi status -qpv
isi services -a
isi devices
isi cluster view
isi network interfaces list
isi auth status

System & drive stats 

isi statistics system list --nodes=all
isi statistics drive list --nodes=all --sort=queued --limit=10
isi statistics heat list --limit 10 --long --event contended,blocked

Version‑specific notes - 9.2+: better scaling under high node counts - 9.3–9.4: improved drive telemetry (busy/queued)

docs.title: Conversions & Utilities
docs.text:
printf '%d
' 0xFFEFF
printf '%x
' 1048319

unix_time=1700000000; date -Iseconds -d @${unix_time}

docs.title: Cores, Panics, Savecore, Hangdumps
docs.text: isi_savecore --list; isi_savecore -d /ifs/data/Isilon_Support/<SR#> --save

docs.title: Drives — Health, Smartfail, Stalls & Boot Flash
docs.text:
List & smartfail 

isi devices drive list --format=table -v
isi devices -a smartfail -d 2:35

Boot flash wear 

isi_for_array -s 'isi_radish -a /dev/ad*' | grep "Percent Life" | grep -v Used

Drive stalls (logs) 

grep 'changed to stalled' */varlog.tar/log/messages | grep -o 'node [0-9]* drive [0-9]* changed to stalled' | sort | uniq -c

⚠️ SAFETY — internal test entry point

NEVER run on L3 SSD; use only when instructed by Support.

isi_test_thread "lbm_drive_stats(<LNUM>)"

docs.title: Elvis Quick Commands Cheats
docs.text:
i, auth, fwchck, gr, dffull, al, alh, boot, core, dflog, disk, dmi, dr, drh,
... (environment helpers)

docs.title: Events, Alerts & Clearing
docs.text:
isi alert list
isi alert cancel <alert_id>

8.x event groups 

isi event groups bulk --ignore=true
isi event groups bulk --resolved=true --ignored=true
isi event groups modify --id=<id> --resolved=true

Version‑specific notes - 8.0+: event group model replaces classic - 9.0+: faster bulk ops, better state

docs.title: FSAnalyze & IndexUpdate (FSA‑IU)
docs.text:
Modes 

isi_gconfig -t job-config jobs.fsa.snap_based_mode=true
isi_gconfig fsa.use_snapshot=false

Remediation 

isi job cancel fsanalyze; isi job cancel indexupdate
isi_gconfig fsa.du_has_dpid=true
isi job start indexupdate; isi job start fsanalyze

Index cleanup 

/usr/bin/isi_index_mod -ll
isi_index_mod -rk cluster_lin_index
isi_changelist_mod -x -l

Version‑specific notes - 8.0: FSA snap debuts - 8.2.1–8.2.2: major stability fixes - 9.x: faster treewalks & better detection

docs.title: Hardware — Nodes — Inventory — Boot
docs.text:
BMC/CMC/CAR 

/usr/bin/isi_hwtools/isi_ipmicmc -d -V -a bmc|cmc|car

DRAM / ECC 

/usr/bin/isi_hwtools/isi_hw_check -s -m safe.id.dram
/usr/bin/isi_hwtools/isi_hw_check -m safe.id.memsize | grep DRAM

⚠️ SAFETY — outage by design

Panic/reboot triggers below cause immediate outage — do not run on production clusters.

isi_rbm_panic <LNN>
kldload reboot_me
kldload panic_me

docs.title: Internal Network — Infiniband & RBM
docs.text:
isi_for_array -sXI 'for i in $(isi_nodes %{name}":"%{internal_a_address}":"%{internal_b_address}); do ... done'

id=$(isi_nodes -L %{id}); sysctl efs.rbm.devices | grep -B4 "sock: 0x0"

Version‑specific notes - 9.1+: clearer RBM device reporting

docs.title: Job Engine — Reports DB, Failpoints & Throttling
docs.text:
Pin reports DB 

isi set -r -g retune -a random -p 3x -F --nodepool <npid> /ifs/.ifsvar/modules/jobengine/reports.db

Reports DB (sqlite) 

cd /ifs/.ifsvar/modules/jobengine
sqlite3 reports.db
select job_id,progress,waiting_on_jid,status from jobs where progress='';

Failpoint 

isi_for_array "isi_ufp -t -C isi_job_d_ufp skip_coord_update_needed_ret='return(0)'"
isi_for_array "isi_ufp -t -C isi_job_d_ufp skip_coord_update_needed_ret='off'"

Throttle tuning (examples) 

isi_gconfig -t job-config impact.profiles.high.fixed_worker_count=1
isi_gconfig -t job-config impact.profiles.low.workers_per_core=0

Version‑specific notes - 8.1–8.2: "database is locked" more common; 9.x reduces frequency - 9.1+: core.load_balance_interval_sec is impactful

docs.title: Job Monitoring & Busy LINs
docs.text:
Busy vnodes 

isi_for_array -s 'for n in $(sysctl efs.bam.busy_vnodes | egrep "isi_job_d" | egrep -o "[[:alnum:]]{1,4}:[[:alnum:]]{4}:[[:alnum:]]{4}" | sort | uniq); do isi get -L "$n"; done'

Continuous watch 

while true; do
  date; isi status -qpv;
  isi_for_array -X sysctl efs.lbm.drive_space | grep blkfree | sort -nk3 -t "=" | head;
  for i in $(isi job list|awk '/^[0-9].*Running/{print $1}'); do
    isi job view $i; isi job statistics view -v --job-id=$i | grep -o 'Workers: [0-9]*' | sort | uniq -c;
  done; sleep 60; echo;
done

Version‑specific notes - 9.2+: higher worker scale - 9.3–9.4: better busy_vnodes correlation

docs.title: Jobs & Job Engine (Core)
docs.text:
Core commands 

isi job list
isi job status <id>
isi job view <id>
isi job start <JobName>
isi job cancel <JobName or --job-id>

⚠️ SAFETY — service disruption risk

Prefer controlled restarts over hard terminations.

Prefer service control (safer) 

isi services -a isi_job_d disable
sleep 60
isi services -a isi_job_d enable

If directed by Support (last resort) 

isi_for_array -s killall -9 isi_job_d

After restart, verify: isi_for_array ps awux | grep job_d

Version‑specific notes - 8.0: JE redesign (coordinator/throttling) - 9.0–9.2: steadier coordinator - 9.3+: better handling of very large snapshots & CloudPools datasets

docs.title: Locking, Vnodes & Performance Signals
docs.text:
isi_for_array -X 'sysctl efs.lin.lock.initiator.oldest_waiter | grep -E "address|started"' | sort -nk4

isi_sysctl_cluster kern.maxvnodes=2000000

while true; do date; sysctl isi.lwext.mbuf_mem isi.lwext.mbuf_data isi.lwext.mbuf_fds kern.mbaudit; sleep 5; done

docs.title: Logs & Elvis Logsets
docs.text:
/mnt/sondgst1nv1/
*-*/varlog.tar/log/messages*
nodes_info

cat <Node>-*/varlog.tar/log/idi.log | grep -Eo '[0-9a-f]:[0-9a-f]{4}:[0-9a-f]{4}::[0-9A-H]{4,}' | sort | uniq

docs.title: Cluster and services
docs.text:
isi status
isi services -a
isi devices
isi statistics query current
isi events list --reduced
isi alert list --show-active

docs.title: Jobs
docs.text:
isi job list
isi job status
isi job view <id>
isi job statistics view --job-id <id> -v

docs.title: Networking quick view
docs.text:
isi networks list interfaces -v
netstat -n | grep 445   # SMB
netstat -n | grep 2049  # NFS

docs.title: Networking (Ethernet) & Packet Capture
docs.text:
isi networks list interfaces -v
isi network groupnets list
isi network subnets list
isi network pools list

ARP 

arp -an; arp -da; arp -an

Cluster‑wide tcpdump 

isi_for_array -X 'for i in $(ifconfig | grep -B2 ether | grep flags | egrep -v "laggport|lo0|ISIINTERNAL" | cut -d: -f1); do   tcpdump -i ${i} -s320 -w /ifs/data/Isilon_Support/`hostname`.${i}_$(date +%m%d%Y_%H%M%S).pcap & done' &>/dev/null &

docs.title: Quotas & Snapshots
docs.text:
isi quotas view /ifs/<path>
isi snap list; isi snap view <id>; isi snap delete <id>

docs.title: Risky Commands (Read First)
docs.text:
⚠️ SAFETY WARNING — High‑risk operations

The commands below can cause service impact, node panics, or data unavailability if misused.

Run only with explicit guidance and maintenance windows, capture pre/post‑state, and have rollback.

  • killall / killall -9 (e.g., isi_job_d) — may abruptly terminate core daemons.
  • isi_test_thread "lbm_drive_stats(<LNUM>)" — NEVER run on L3 SSD; internal testing only.
  • isi_rbm_panic, kldload reboot_me, kldload panic_me — force reboot/panic; outage by design.

docs.title: Scripting, Parsers & One‑liners
docs.text:
find . -type f -print0 | xargs -0 stat -f "%m %N" | sort -rn | head -1 | cut -f2- -d" "

awk '{print $(NF-1)}'

docs.title: Network port no carrier (external)
docs.text:
1. Check cables/ports
  2. Swap cable/port; review switch logs
  3. Validate pool config (expectations)

Helpful outputs 

isi networks list interfaces -v
netstat -an; ifconfig -a; pciconf -lv

docs.title: Upgrades, Patches & Firmware
docs.text:
isi upgrade view
isi upgrade nodes list
isi_upgrade_status
isi_upgrade_logs

Retry last action 

isi upgrade cluster retry-last-action --nodes=3

Patch/Package 

isi_for_array -s 'isi_patch list'
isi upgrade patches list
isi_for_array -s tail -30 /var/log/isi_pkg
isi pkg info --forced_local
isi pkg delete

Firmware 

isi upgrade cluster firmware devices
rm /var/ifs/upgrade/firmware_status.json

Version‑specific notes - 8.2+: clearer upgrade logs - 9.5–9.7: some drive FW rollouts may need a reboot to complete

docs.title: Version Matrix (7.x → 9.7)
docs.text: Area 7.x 8.0–8.2 9.0–9.7 Job Engine Classic Major redesign; snap-based FSA Faster, more stable; big snapshot scale FSAnalyze/IU N/A FSA snap debuts; 8.2.x stability Faster treewalk; better detection Drives/Stalls Limited logs Stall events clearer Expanded stall patterns; FW rollouts Networking SMB1/NFS3 focus SMB2/3 matures Lower latency spikes (Gen7)
